headline: CONFIRMATION (anti-double-counting). April 23 layoff of 10% workforce (~8,000 employees, effective May 20; 6,000 cancelled open roles; AI infrastructure $135B capex offset rationale; concentrated in content moderation, customer support, software testing, certain engineering) was already absorbed in April 28 confirmation cycle at 29.4. No new acute April 24-29 net evidence. Per anti-double-counting rule, re-assessing the same April 23 announcement does not generate a new proposal. Composite 29.4 (Developing) holds.

evidence.finding: 10% of workforce (~8,000) layoff announced effective May 20; 6,000 open roles cancelled; AI infrastructure $135B capex offset rationale.
Cuts concentrated in content moderation, customer support, software testing, certain engineering — AI automation explicit enabler.
Combined with Microsoft, 20K total cuts framed as AI-driven labor crisis. Meta mechanism is direct layoff (more coercive than Microsoft buyout).

negativeSignals: Direct (non-voluntary) layoff at 10% scale; content moderation reduction has direct downstream user-safety implications (BND); AI-displacement rationale.
anti_double_counting_note: April 23 layoff was the basis for April 28 confirmation at 29.4. Per anti-double-counting rule, no new proposal generated unless materially new evidence emerges beyond the April 23 announcement.
